sectionOrderRules: Hero must state what the farm or business offers and include a primary CTA.
Offering summary must describe actual products, farm sales or services, not generic marketing claims.
Trust proof should use real signals such as farm shop, organic labeling if provided, local presence or open farm activities.
Opening hours and map preview may appear before the final contact CTA when visit traffic is important.
Sections should reflect seasonality when the dossier includes relevant information and must not promise availability that is unknown.
Products intro must explain what visitors can buy or book and how purchasing works.
Product list must be concrete, for example vegetables, eggs, meat, plants, fodder or farm-related offers, and should avoid vague labels.
Pricing is optional and should only be shown when real prices or price examples exist.
If the page represents farm shop sales, opening hours should be visible on the page when available.
About story must be specific to the farm, land, production, hunting context or support operations.
Team section may represent family, owners or staff and should stay concise for smaller operations.
Trust proof must rely on factual credentials, labels or local practices supplied in the dossier and must not invent certifications.
Contact info must include available real contact methods and a physical location when relevant for visits or deliveries.
Map and opening hours should be shown when the business receives visitors on site, runs a farm shop or expects pickup.
Contact CTA should support simple enquiries about products, visits, availability or services.
